title: Triage 5 catalog tools through the gate
context: The candidate-software catalog (catalog-005) provides a pool of tools to evaluate. Before any contribution can begin for a given tool, it must pass the gate (gate-002). This triage task runs five catalog tools through both gate axes — software license + contribution policy, and cancer-data guardrail — and produces a committed PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE artifact for each, confirming which tools are ready for M1 contributions. riskTier is medium because evaluating data-handling for some tools (Priority B/C with external datasets or COSMIC/OncoKB adjacency) requires nuanced licence/de-identification judgment.
objective: Apply gate-002 to five tools from the candidate-software catalog, producing a committed PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE artifact for each, identifying which tools are approved for M1 contribution tasks (contrib-011, contrib-012, contrib-013).
acceptanceCriteria: Five tools from the catalog are evaluated, each with a committed gate artifact covering both axes: PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE status + rationale for the software axis (license, contribution policy, for-profit-vehicle check) and the cancer-data guardrail axis (data-minimization disposition, COSMIC/OncoKB rule, de-identification status).
Each PASS record documents: license id/URL, contribution policy (CLA/DCO), maintenance status, and the data-minimization disposition (shipped/synthetic/open-access).

Any non-OSI, broad-CLA, open-core, or non-redistributable-data case is FLAGGED or EXCLUDED with the specific concern surfaced and the COSMIC/OncoKB 'never embed' rule explicitly applied where relevant.
The five committed artifacts are the direct inputs for selecting tools in contrib-011, contrib-012, and contrib-013.
License+Data reviewer sign-off obtained on all five gate artifacts.
resources: PLAN.md — Data, licensing & compliance (Axis 1 and Axis 2)
SPDX license list; OSI-approved license list; COSMIC/OncoKB terms
output: Five committed gate artifacts (PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE with full rationale) for five catalog tools, identifying the approved targets for M1 contribution tasks.
